footer.tagline: Complete Security, Network & Staff Monitoring Solution for modern IT teams.

home.metaTitle: Log Monitor — Security, Network & Staff Monitoring Solution
home.metaDescription: A complete IT management platform combining security monitoring, network management, hardware asset tracking, staff monitoring, and support ticketing in one dashboard.
home.heroTitle: Complete Security, Network & Staff Monitoring Solution
home.heroSubtitle: A single platform to monitor your security posture, network health, hardware assets, and staff activity — with real-time alerts, role-based access, and a support ticket system built in.
home.aboutIntro: A centralized platform covering everything IT teams need to keep infrastructure secure, visible, and well-managed.
home.ctaTitle: Ready to secure and simplify your IT infrastructure?
home.ctaSubtitle: Sign in to your dashboard or get in touch with our team.

aboutSoftware.metaDescription: A complete IT management platform: security monitoring, network management, hardware asset tracking, staff monitoring, alerts, reporting, and ticketing.
aboutSoftware.title: About the Software
aboutSoftware.intro: Log Monitor brings together everything an IT team needs to keep infrastructure secure, visible, and well-managed — real-time monitoring, hardware inventory, staff activity oversight, and a built-in support ticket system, all from one dashboard.

security.title: Security Monitoring
security.description: Continuous visibility into firewall events, threats, and system security posture across your infrastructure.

network.title: Network Monitoring
network.description: Real-time tracking of routers, bandwidth usage, connected devices, and network health.

hardware.title: Hardware Asset Management
hardware.description: A complete inventory of computers, servers, and endpoint hardware, including specs and health status.

staff.title: Staff Activity Monitoring
staff.description: Consent-based visibility into staff device activity, with privacy controls built in from the ground up.

alerts.title: System Alerts and Notifications
alerts.description: Instant alerts when thresholds are crossed — high CPU, disk failures, security events, and more.

dashboard.title: Dashboard Reporting
dashboard.description: Clear, real-time dashboards summarizing the health and status of your entire IT environment.

userRoles.title: User and Role Management
userRoles.description: Control who can access what, with role-based permissions across the platform.

tickets.title: Ticket Management
tickets.description: A built-in support ticket system so issues get tracked, assigned, and resolved.

audit.title: Audit Logs
audit.description: A full trail of who did what and when — critical for accountability and compliance.

auth.title: Secure Authentication
auth.description: Encrypted credentials and session management to keep access to your systems locked down.

aboutUs.metaDescription: Our company, vision, and mission behind the Log Monitor IT management platform.
aboutUs.title: About Our Company
aboutUs.intro: We provide comprehensive IT management solutions designed to help organizations efficiently manage their technology infrastructure.
aboutUs.combined: Our platform combines Security Monitoring, Network Management, Hardware Asset Tracking, Staff Monitoring, and Support Ticket Management into one centralized system.
aboutUs.missionStatement: Our mission is to improve security, increase operational efficiency, and simplify IT administration through an easy-to-use, secure, and scalable platform.
aboutUs.vision: To become a trusted provider of secure and intelligent IT management solutions that empower organizations to protect their digital infrastructure and improve productivity.

missionPoints.0: Deliver secure and reliable IT solutions

support.title: Support
support.intro: Find answers below, or submit a support ticket and our team will help you directly.
support.knowledgeBaseDesc: Guides and articles covering the platform's core features.

guides.0: Getting started with the monitoring dashboard
guides.2: Submitting and tracking a support ticket

access.question: How do I get access to the dashboard?
access.answer: Dashboard access is provisioned by your IT administrator. Contact support if you need an account.

report.question: How do I report an issue?
report.answer: Submit a support ticket with as much detail as possible, including a screenshot if relevant.

checkStatus.question: How can I check the status of a ticket I submitted?
checkStatus.answer: Use the "Check Ticket Status" page with your ticket number and the email you submitted with.

consent.question: Is staff monitoring done without consent?
consent.answer: No — endpoint monitoring only begins after explicit local consent is given on the device itself, and is limited to company-owned devices under written policy.

support.stillNeedHelp: Still need help? Email us at

maintenance.title: Under Maintenance
maintenance.defaultMessage: We're currently performing scheduled maintenance. Please check back shortly.

dashboardHome.title: Overview
dashboardHome.subtitle: Sophos firewall · MikroTik router · System health & web filter monitoring
dashboardHome.deviceFirstSeen: {name}''s device first seen on the network

list.intro: An employee is mapped to a MAC address, not an IP — the IP shown below is just the device's current address, resolved live from the MikroTik router's DHCP leases or this server's ARP table for Sophos-side clients. Every IP that MAC has ever used is kept, so the activity report on each employee's page covers their full history even after an IP changes — not just today. Everything here (Employees, device history, Web Filter, Router logs) is stored permanently in the SQL database, nothing is held only in memory. Online/Offline and First Seen are based on network activity only — there's no OS login time, and CPU/RAM/screen data isn't available without an endpoint agent installed on each PC.
list.filterByDeviceTypeHint: Filter by device type (click a tile; click again to clear):

errors.duplicateMac: That MAC address is already assigned to another employee.

detail.noPcLinkedNotice: No PC linked yet. Install the endpoint agent on this employee's computer, then set <link>that device's</link> "Assigned staff member" field to this employee to enable per-employee reports (CPU, RAM, screenshots, software, processes).
detail.ipHistoryCount: This MAC address has used {count} different IPs over time (DHCP renewals) — the report below covers activity across all of them.
detail.noDeviceEverSeenNotice: No device currently assigned or ever seen for this staff member, so no activity to show.
detail.activityReportSub: Across all known IPs for this device, most recent first (up to 50 shown).
detail.noWebFilterEvents: No Web Filter events for this device yet.
detail.noRouterWebConnections: No router web connections for this device yet.

backupData.title: Backup and Data
backupData.dataRestoreDescription: Restoring a `.bak` file over a live, in-use database is destructive and can corrupt the running application if done incorrectly. This phase intentionally does not expose a one-click restore button. To restore: stop the application pool, then run RESTORE DATABASE against the desired backup file directly in SQL Server Management Studio (or sqlcmd), then restart the application pool.
backupData.scheduleNotWiredUpNotice: Schedule is saved for reference — automatic execution isn't wired up yet. Use "Run Database Backup Now" above in the meantime.
backupData.retentionNotesPlaceholder: Notes about what this retention policy applies to
backupData.confirmBackupMessage: This runs a full BACKUP DATABASE against the live database. It may take a moment on a large database.
backupData.scheduleSavedToast: Backup schedule saved. Automatic execution is coming in a future update — trigger backups manually below until then.
